title: Licence Grant
description: The consent/entitlement link between a credential holder (by email) and a tenant that may see their licences. This is what makes employer access a *grant*, not ownership: the employee owns the credential; a tenant sees it because a grant exists. An employer that asserts records on a worker's behalf gets an 'implied' grant; once the employee onboards to Cards and claims, they control it (consented/revoked) and can extend grants to other tenants — portability. Holder-keyed by email so it survives across employers.

licence_grant_id.description: Internal canonical ID. Generated on insert if not provided.

tenant_id.description: The tenant granted visibility of this holder's credentials. Set by context.

holder_email.description: The credential holder this grant concerns — the portable identity anchor (matches licence.holder_email).

status.description: implied = employer asserted records on the worker's behalf and holds visibility pre-consent; consented = the employee has onboarded/claimed and explicitly approved this tenant; revoked = the employee withdrew access. Operational access requires implied OR consented.

granted_by.description: How the grant arose. employer_assertion = created when an employer imported/asserted records; employee_consent = created/upgraded when the employee approved a share request.

scope.description: What the grant covers. all_licences = the holder's whole wallet (current default); future: scope to specific licence types.

requested_at.description: When a share was requested (employee-consent flow). Null for implied employer grants.

responded_at.description: When the employee approved/revoked. Null until they act.
